title: Kannamoochi Yenada Lyrics Kandukondain Kandukondain
stanzas:
Kannamoochi yenada
en kannaa naan
kannaadi porul polada(2)

andha nadhiyin karaiyai
naan kaetten.
andha kaatrai niruthiyum
kaetten. (2)

Kannamoochi yenada
en kannaa naan
kannaadi porul polada

en manam unakkoru
vilaiyaattu bommaiyaa(2)
enakkena unarchigal
thaniyaaga illaiyaa
nenjin alai urangaadhu

un imai kondu
vizhi mooda vaa
un udal thaan
en udai allavaa

en nenjil koodiye
niram maaravaa
ennuyiril nee vandhu serga
udhadugal eeramaai
vazhga kalandhidavaa.

Kannamoochi yenada
en kannaa naan
kannaadi porul polada

Vaan mazhai vizhumpodhu
malai kondu kaappaai.(2)
kan mazhai vizhum podhu
edhil ennai kaappaai

poovin kanneerai rasippai
naan enna pennilaiyaa
kannaa adhai nee kaana
kannillaiyaa
un kanavugalil
naan illaiyaa

dhinam oosal aaduthu manasu
ada unmai alla en kolusu
en ullmoochile uyir neengudhe
ennuyir thudikkaamale
kaappadhu un theendale
uyir thara vaa...

Kannamoochi yenada
en kannaa naan
kannaadi porul polada

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kannamoochi Yenada?

The song is a romantic melody that explores themes of love and longing.

In what context does the song appear in Kandukondain Kandukondain?

The song is featured in a romantic setting, highlighting the emotions between the lead characters.

Who are the singers of Kannamoochi Yenada?

The song is beautifully sung by K.J. Yesudas and K.S. Chitra.

Who composed the music for Kandukondain Kandukondain?

The music was composed by the legendary A.R. Rahman.

When was Kannamoochi Yenada released?

The song was released in the year 2000.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In the movie Kandukondain Kandukondain, the song Kannamoochi Yenada is a beautiful romantic duet that captures the essence of love and longing between the lead characters. Sung by K.J. Yesudas and K.S. Chitra, the song is set against a backdrop of emotional depth and connection. A.R. Rahman's composition adds a soothing and nostalgic quality, making it a timeless classic. The lyrics convey a sense of yearning and affection, evoking deep emotions in the listeners.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the enchanting melody of Kannamoochi Yenada from the timeless film Kandukondain Kandukondain. This beautiful duet by K.J. Yesudas and K.S. Chitra, composed by the legendary A.R. Rahman, captures the essence of romance and nostalgia. Dive into a world of soothing tunes and heartfelt lyrics that resonate with love and longing.
stanzaMeanings: Why are you hiding from me?
Why are you hiding from me, my dear?
Why are you hiding from me, my dear? I am like a mirror to you.
I asked for the riverbank. I also asked to stop the wind.
I asked for the open sky, but there is no answer.
In the end, I found you in the flower of my heart.
Why are you hiding from me, my dear? I am like a mirror to you.
Is my heart just a toy for you? Don't I have feelings of my own? My heart's waves don't rest.
Come and close my mouth with your lips, my dear.
Come and close my eyes with your eyelids. Isn't your body my dress?
Even after playing in the ocean, your color hasn't changed.
Will you not change color in my heart? Come join my life, let our lips live together.
Why are you hiding from me, my dear? I am like a mirror to you.
When the rain falls from the sky, you protect with a mountain. When tears fall from my eyes, what will protect me?
You enjoy the tears of flowers. Am I not a woman, my dear? Can't you see that? Am I not in your dreams?
The heart swings every day, but it's not true. My anklet, in my breath, life is leaving. Without my heart beating, your touch saves me, give me life.
Why are you hiding from me, my dear? I am like a mirror to you.
